Title: Prokaryotic Diacylglycerol Kinase and Undecaprenol Kinase

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Does not function as undecaprenol kinase; instead dephosphorylates undecaprenyl pyrophosphate
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bacA in E. coli was long thought to be an undecaprenol kinase. 2. The review states it was recently shown to be an undecaprenyl pyrophosphatase. 3. Therefore bacA is not the dgkA-like UDPK discussed in this paper.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: prior biochemical reassignment cited in review
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a functionally contrasted enzyme in undecaprenoid recycling, not a main experimental subject of this review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides diacylglycerol kinase activity required for recycling DAG produced during lipoteichoic acid production in Gram-positive bacteria
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Many Gram-positive bacteria encode a second, soluble DAGK homolog termed dgkB. 2. In Bacillus subtilis, dgkB was shown to encode a functional DAGK distinct from dgkA/UDPK. 3. dgkB provides critical DAG recycling during lipoteichoic acid production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional characterization and structural study cited in review
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important contextual gene for distinguishing Gram-positive UDPK function from DAGK function.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Escherichia coli; Streptococcus mutans; Bacillus subtilis; Staphylococcus aureus
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Escherichia coli; Streptococcus mutans; Bacillus subtilis; Staphylococcus aureus
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Escherichia coli; Streptococcus mutans; Bacillus subtilis; Staphylococcus aureus
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Small multispan membrane kinase family; homotrimeric membrane enzyme with three transmembrane helices per subunit
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Diacylglycerol recycling in glycerophospholipid metabolism; undecaprenol recycling linked to cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MDO-cycle DAG recycling; LPS-biosynthesis DAG recycling; undecaprenol-to-undecaprenyl-phosphate recycling branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: sn-1,2-diacylglycerol (E. coli DAGK) or undecaprenol (Gram-positive UDPK); ATP as phosphoryl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the terminal phosphate from ATP to diacylglycerol to form phosphatidic acid in Gram-negative bacteria, or to undecaprenol to form undecaprenyl phosphate in many Gram-positive bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as classic rate-limiting enzyme; described as critical recycling enzyme whose catalytic rate roughly matches substrate diffusion/flip-flop in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In Gram-negative bacteria, recycles membrane-disruptive DAG into phosphatidic acid; in many Gram-positive bacteria, phosphorylates undecaprenol to support cell wall-related metabolism, acid resistance, lantibiotic biosynthesis, biofilm formation, and sporulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: phosphatidic acid supports phospholipid biosynthesis and DAG detoxification; undecaprenyl phosphate is a lipid carrier for peptidoglycan and teichoic-acid/cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In E. coli, dgkA transcription is induced under stringent response and by basRS signaling; downregulated by sigmaE during heat stress while plsB is activated
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low osmolarity medium activates MDO pathway in E. coli; acidic culture conditions are relevant to S. mutans UDPK physiology; heat stress and stringent response affect E. coli dgkA regulation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BasR/BasS signaling system; functionally linked to PlsB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: A second promoter for plsB is discussed; specific dgkA promoter names not given
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic mapping; gene sequencing; knockout/conditional phenotype studies; enzyme purification and reconstitution; substrate specificity assays; kinetic studies; NMR structure and substrate titration; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target, especially Gram-positive UDPK involved in virulence and biofilm formation in Streptococcus mutans; not a crop nutrient-engineering target
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dgkA encodes a small membrane kinase family that functions as DAGK in Gram-negative bacteria and as UDPK in many Gram-positive bacteria. 2. In E. coli, dgkA recycles DAG generated during membrane-derived oligosaccharide and lipopolysaccharide biosynthesis by converting DAG to phosphatidic acid. 3. In Streptococcus mutans and Bacillus subtilis, dgkA functions primarily as undecaprenol kinase, producing undecaprenyl phosphate. 4. Gram-positive dgkA/UDPK is important for acid resistance, biofilm formation, lantibiotic biosynthesis, virulence, and sporulation. 5. E. coli DAGK is a structurally unique homotrimeric membrane enzyme and a model for membrane protein catalysis, folding, and misfolding.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review article; dgkA is the principal summarized core pathway gene. Function differs by species and should not be merged mechanistically beyond family-level description.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Response regulator in two-component signaling system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulates dgkA transcription, thereby promoting recycling of DAG generated during lipopolysaccharide biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports phospholipid recycling/homeostasis during LPS-associated DAG production
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: DAG recycling linked to LPS biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: two-component signaling; associated with LPS biosynthesis control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Provides regulatory link that elevates dgkA expression when DAG recycling is needed during LPS biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antibacterial regulatory target but not relevant to crop nutrient breeding
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. basR was identified as the long-unidentified dgkR locus. 2. basR-originating two-component signaling upregulates dgkA transcription in E. coli. 3. This regulation connects DAG recycling to LPS biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review explicitly identifies basR/dgkR as a regulator of the focal metabolic gene dgkA.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heat stress-induced sigmaE activates plsB transcription and downregulates dgkA, reciprocally modulating phospholipid biosynthesis versus DAG recycling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of plsB and transcriptional downregulation of dgkA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Balance between de novo phospholipid biosynthesis and DAG recycling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Shifts flux regulation between phosphatidic acid synthesis via PlsB pathway and DAG recycling via DgkA under heat stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sigmaE is heat stress induced in E. coli. 2. It activates plsB transcription while downregulating dgkA. 3. This establishes reciprocal regulation between de novo phospholipid synthesis and DAG recycling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background regulator relevant to dgkA-centered metabolic control, not a primary subject of structural enzymology sections.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Operon-associated stress-responsive factor linked to UDPK pathway; suggests coupling of dgkA/UDPK to stress-induced modulation of ribosome function under acidic conditions
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall biosynthesis carrier lipid precursor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: stress-responsive membrane/RNA association; indirect pathway linkage
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: putative stress-induced pathway associated with UDPK and ribosome function
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not shown to directly regulate undecaprenoid metabolism, but operon association implies functional coupling to UDPK pathway in stress adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ribosomal RNA; dgkA operon components; ygfG
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane-associated or RNA-associated depending on stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: acidic culture conditions linked to membrane association
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: operon/contextual genetic association and prior functional studies cited in review
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential anti-cariogenic target context, but indirect
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The Streptococcus mutans dgkA gene is often located in a three-gene operon including sgp. 2. Sgp is an Era-like small GTPase involved in stress-responsive ribosome-related functions. 3. Its association with dgkA suggests UDPK participates in a stress-induced pathway affecting ribosome function, though direct metabolic regulation remains unproven.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included cautiously as an operon-associated regulator rather than a direct validated controller of metabolite flux.
